input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.question: How many venues are involved in the main tournament which is part of the English Cricket Team's 2017-2018 schedule?
input_dict.current_output: England will take part in a "three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament" co‑hosted by Australia and New Zealand (English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18; 2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series). Neither of those passages lists or counts the tournament "Venues," so the number of venues cannot be determined from the provided sources.

upstream_context.question: How many venues are involved in the main tournament which is part of the English Cricket Team's 2017-2018 schedule?
upstream_context.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18 — "take part in a three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament" ; same passage — "New Zealand ... will co-host the tournament along with Australia."  
Bridge needed: the tournament page for the 2017–18 three‑nation T20I (England–Australia–New Zealand) tri-series to verify the list/count of venues.  
Retrieval direction: 1. Search for the tournament page on Wikipedia (query: "England New Zealand Australia T20 tri-series 2017 site:wikipedia.org"). 2. Fetch the tournament page and inspect the 'Venues' (or 'Matches'/'Schedule') section to count distinct venues used in the main tournament.
hop2_titles: English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18
hop2_docs: 2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series | The 2017–18 Trans-Tasman Tri-Series is an upcoming Twenty20 International (T20I) cricket tournament scheduled to be held in Australia and New Zealand in February 2018.  It is a tri-nation series between Australia, England and New Zealand.  It follows on from England's tour of Australia, which includes the 2017–18 Ashes series, and takes the place of the planned Chappell–Hadlee Trophy series.

2017 Blind T20 World Cup | The 2017 Blind World T20 is a T20I tournament held for the blind cricketers in India for the second time from 30 January to 12 February.India defeated Pakistan by 9 wickets in the finals to win their second ever Blind T20 World Cup.  Ten teams such as hosts India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, England, Bangladesh, West Indies, South Africa, Nepal, Australia and New Zealand played in 48 matches.Each team had to play against all the teams which are competing for the title.

2017–18 Ashes series | The 2017–18 Ashes series is a forthcoming series of Test cricket matches to be contested between England and Australia for The Ashes.  The series will be played at five venues across Australia between 23 November 2017 and 8 January 2018.

English cricket team in Australia in 2017–18 | The England cricket team is scheduled to tour Australia between November 2017 and February 2018 to play five Tests and five One Day Internationals (ODIs).  They will also take part in a three nation Twenty20 International (T20I) tournament, along with New Zealand, who will co-host the tournament along with Australia.  The Test series will form part of the 2017–18 Ashes series.  In May 2017, it was confirmed that the WACA Ground would host the Test and ODI in Perth, as the planned new ground was unable to open in time.

2017 Ireland Tri-Nation Series | The 2017 Ireland Tri-Nation Series was a One Day International cricket tournament that took place in Ireland in May 2017.  It was a tri-nation series between Ireland, Bangladesh and New Zealand.  The matches were in preparation for the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy, which is scheduled to take place in June 2017 in England and Wales.  Cricket Ireland announced the full fixtures in July 2016.  Ahead of the ODI fixtures, Ireland played two warm-up matches; a 50-over match against Bangladesh and a 25-over match against New Zealand.

2017–18 Senior women's T20 league | 2017–18 Senior women's T20 league was the tenth edition of the Senior women's T20 league, a domestic women's T20 competition in India.  It will be held from 12 January to 27 January 2017.

Toka Natua | Toka Natua (born 22 November 1991) is a rugby union player from New Zealand.  She made her debut for the New Zealand women's national rugby union team, the Black Ferns, in 2015 against Canada. .  Natua scored three tries in New Zealand's win over England in the final of the 2017 Women's Rugby World Cup.  She was also cited for foul play in the 2017 cup final against England.
